sn.s: Geld und Zahle sind im Alltag wichtig.
sn.e: Money and numbers are important in daily life.
A franc has a hundred rappen.

sn.s: Mer redt vo 'Stutz' für Franke und 'Rappe' fürs Chli.
sn.e: People say 'Stutz' for francs and 'Rappe' for the small change.

sn.s: Im Lade frag ich: "Wieviel macht das zäme?"
sn.e: In the shop I ask: "How much is that altogether?"
I give her a fifty-franc note.
She gives me the change back.

sn.s: Z'Luzärn isch s'Läbe nöd grad billig.
sn.e: In Lucerne life isn't exactly cheap.

sn.s: Drum lueg ich, dass ich es bitzli spare.
sn.e: So I make sure to save a little.

sn.s: Aber gueti Sache dörfed au öppis koschte.
sn.e: But good things are allowed to cost something.

sn.s: Langsam verstah ich d'Zahle bim Rede.
sn.e: Slowly I understand the numbers when spoken.

sn.s: Mit Geld umgah uf Schwiizerdütsch: gschafft.
sn.e: Handling money in Swiss German: managed.
